labs.title: Action Graph & Human Authority
labs.value: Shows why GoalOS prepares action, but does not self-authorize high-impact action.
labs.bestFor: Executives, reviewers, compliance teams, AI delivery leads.

labs.title: Proof-Carrying Artifact & Evolution Ledger
labs.value: Shows how reusable capability earns the right to influence future work.
labs.bestFor: Product teams, AI platform teams, governance stewards.

labs.title: Independent Replay & Claim Promotion
labs.value: Shows that one run is not proof; replay makes a claim institutionally usable.
labs.bestFor: Research reviewers, auditors, grant evaluators, technical diligence teams.

labs.title: ProofZero Planning & Evidence Reanalyze
labs.value: Shows how GoalOS plans over proof-relevant work states instead of persuasive futures.
labs.bestFor: AI operators, mission designers, evaluation teams.

labs.title: Proof-Gated Mission Foundry & Curriculum
labs.value: Shows how accepted proof becomes curriculum rather than a dead artifact.
labs.bestFor: Program owners, frontier evaluation teams, autonomous work architects.

labs.title: Process-Resolved Evidence
labs.value: Shows that final output is not enough; process-resolved evidence determines review readiness.
labs.bestFor: Corporate reviewers, analysts, legal/compliance observers, AI assurance teams.

labs.title: Blockchain Credibility Standard
labs.value: Shows why credible blockchain projects need proof packages before trust, reputation, governance, or settlement readiness.
labs.bestFor: Blockchain teams, DAOs, foundations, auditors, validators, investors, users.

labs.title: Blockchain Proof Mandate & Due Diligence
labs.value: Turns the blockchain credibility standard into a concrete requirement everyone can ask for, score, and enforce.
labs.bestFor: Users, DAO delegates, grant committees, treasury councils, auditors, investors, partners, enterprises.

labs.title: Proof Before Settlement Research Standard
labs.value: Turns the Proof Before Settlement paper into a public standard stakeholders can read, download, cite, and require.
labs.bestFor: Foundations, DAOs, auditors, investors, exchanges, enterprises, partners.

labs.title: Executive AI Proof Console
labs.value: Makes GoalOS obvious to first-time visitors with a role-based, public-safe proof gate.
labs.bestFor: Executives, DAO delegates, auditors, investors, enterprises, AI operators.

labs.title: From Loop to RSI
labs.value: Connects proof loops to deterministic invention governance and RSI readiness.
labs.bestFor: Frontier labs, governance teams, sovereign strategy leads.

labs.title: Loop → RSI → ASI Console
labs.value: Shows the superintelligence control boundary: promotion cannot self-authorize.
labs.bestFor: Safety leads, architecture councils, frontier lab directors.

labs.title: ASI Superintelligence Control Tower
labs.value: Runs explanation modes, stress tests, rollback drills, council review, and Move‑37 dossier handling.
labs.bestFor: Boards, councils, sovereign programs, assurance teams.

labs.title: ASI Mission Simulator
labs.value: Experiences the complete Loop → RSI → ASI path in one guided, button-only interactive lab.
labs.bestFor: Everyone: first-time visitors, executives, reviewers, blockchain teams, and RSI/ASI governance audiences.

roles.label: First-time visitor
roles.headline: Understand the big idea in one guided path.
roles.brief: Start with the command center. It explains the proof loop, shows what to inspect next, and routes you to the right lab.

roles.label: Blockchain / DAO
roles.headline: Require proof before trust or settlement.
roles.brief: Use v28-v30 to make proof packages normal for grants, treasuries, upgrades, audits, partners, and public claims.

roles.label: Executive / board
roles.headline: Turn complexity into an institutional decision record.
roles.brief: Use the executive console and command center to see the gates, risks, authority, and receipts without reading every artifact first.

roles.label: Auditor / reviewer
roles.headline: Follow the evidence, replay path, and claim boundary.
roles.brief: Open the replay and process evidence labs when the main question is whether the claim can be checked independently.

roles.label: Frontier lab / RSI team
roles.headline: Govern invention before it compounds.
roles.brief: Use v32-v35 to inspect deterministic invention operations, Move‑37 dossiers, stress gates, rollback, and ASI-readiness.

roles.label: Public reviewer
roles.headline: Ask the simple public question: where is the proof package?
roles.brief: Use the proof mandate and route catalog to check whether claims are marketing-only or evidence-ready.

modes.label: Start here
modes.message: GoalOS starts by making a claim inspectable. Define the mission, bind evidence, replay it, validate it, preserve human judgment, and issue a receipt.

modes.label: Proof before settlement
modes.message: For blockchain, the rule is simple: do not just settle transactions; settle proven work.

modes.label: Loop → RSI
modes.message: The proof loop becomes RSI governance when exploration is deterministic, replayable, schema-bound, and gated before promotion.

modes.label: RSI → ASI readiness
modes.message: The ASI boundary is not a capability claim. It is the governance rule: ASI-scale systems must not self-authorize.

boundary: No forms · no text inputs · no uploads · no cookies · no analytics · no wallets · no payments · no external AI calls · no personal or confidential data · zero value moved.
